name: Mendo
why: Mendo is focused on scaling enterprise AI adoption in Europe, positioning them as an AI-native transformation boutique.
evidence: Secures €12M to scale enterprise AI adoption.

name: Prometheus AI
why: Prometheus AI offers AI automation services and deploys AI agents for business functions like sales, reception, and workflow automation, acting as an embedded AI team for mid-size firms.
evidence: Provides AI agents for business that scale operations 24/7.

name: Infinity Constellation
why: Infinity Constellation is an AI-native holding company that builds and operates full-stack AI service businesses to automate and replace traditional professional services.
evidence: Raises $24M Series A funding.

name: Happiest Minds
why: Happiest Minds, a digital engineering and IT company, launched Rel(AI)Build, an agentic AI platform to transform enterprise software delivery, making them a significant player in AI transformation.
evidence: Launches Rel(AI)Build, an Agentic AI Platform.

name: Poetic
why: Poetic is focused on automating complex enterprise processes with reliable AI, aiming to fix issues where AI agents currently fail, indicating a platform for robust agentic deployment.
evidence: Raises $50M Series A funding.

name: Jedify
why: Jedify is building a 'context layer' to provide enterprise AI agents with the necessary information to function effectively, addressing a foundational need for agent deployment.
evidence: Raises $24M funding to build the context layer for enterprise AI agents.

name: ChatSee.ai
why: ChatSee.ai is tackling the problem of AI agent failures, suggesting a platform for monitoring, debugging, or optimizing AI agents for reliable operation.
evidence: Raises $6.5M funding to tackle AI agent failures.

name: Recursive
why: Recursive is focused on self-improving AI where models build models, representing a foundational advancement that could significantly impact future agentic systems and AI transformation.
evidence: Secures $650 Million in funding.

name: GenerativeX
why: GenerativeX secured JPY 650m in Series A funding to accelerate global AI agent deployment, indicating a focus on platforms for deploying AI agents.
evidence: Secured JPY 650m Series A funding on 2026-06-08.

name: Relai
why: Relai raised $6.9M to develop a platform enabling verifiable and continuous learning for AI agents, addressing a key challenge in robust agentic deployment.
evidence: Raises $6.9M funding on 2026-06-10.

name: PhoenixAI
why: PhoenixAI raised $80M in Series B funding to power an agentic AI database, providing a foundational layer for AI agent operations.
evidence: Raises $80M Series B funding on 2026-06-11.

name: 2X
why: 2X acquired Knownwell to create the first GTM human-agentic services company, unifying B2B marketing, sales, and customer success with AI agents.
evidence: Acquires Knownwell on 2026-06-10 to build GTM human-agentic services.

name: Sedai
why: Sedai launched an autonomous platform for AI agent optimization, providing tools to intelligently route and manage AI agents from various models.
evidence: Launches autonomous platform on 2026-06-09.

name: Centerprise AI
why: Centerprise AI introduced its platform for the agentic evolution of data integration and management, providing critical infrastructure for enterprise AI agents.
evidence: Introduced on 2026-06-10.

name: Decisions
why: Decisions unveiled a new brand to power the missing control layer for enterprise AI, helping orchestrate AI agents, people, and systems at scale.
evidence: Unveiled new brand on 2026-06-09.

name: OpenAI DeployCo
why: OpenAI DeployCo is a new, well-funded initiative by OpenAI to directly provide enterprise AI consulting and deployment services, including embedding 'Forward Deployed Engineers' to help integrate AI into workflows.
evidence: Launched with $4 billion in funding and acquired AI consulting firm Tomoro.

name: Conduct
why: Conduct offers an AI-driven platform that uses agentic AI to optimize and modernize legacy ERP systems, starting with SAP, by providing visibility into customizations and accelerating system changes.
evidence: Raised $60 million in Series A funding.

name: Sarvam AI
why: Sarvam AI is a full-stack generative AI platform building language technologies and AI agents specifically for Indian languages, enabling multilingual AI solutions and conversational agents for various sectors.
evidence: Achieved $1.5 billion valuation with $234 million Series B funding.

name: Zensar Technologies (ZenseAI.AgentMesh)
why: Zensar's ZenseAI.AgentMesh is a new platform offering a universal operating system for agentic AI, with pre-built agents and governance features to accelerate enterprise AI adoption and deployment.
evidence: Launched ZenseAI.AgentMesh, an enterprise-grade agentic AI platform.

name: Zaro.ai
why: Zaro.ai provides a platform to unify enterprise AI agents, data, and applications within a single context layer, facilitating more cohesive and effective agent deployments.
evidence: Secured $5.1 million in funding.

name: Konecta (Kolibri)
why: Konecta's Kolibri is an agentic AI orchestration platform designed to accelerate enterprise AI deployment by offering pre-built, sector-specific use cases and governance, leveraging their extensive CX expertise.
evidence: Launched Kolibri, an agentic AI orchestration platform.

name: Cognizant (Neuro AI Multi-Agent Accelerator)
why: Cognizant's Neuro AI Multi-Agent Accelerator is a platform that enables enterprises to orchestrate AI agents from different vendors (like ServiceNow) and custom systems within a single, unified environment.
evidence: Launched Neuro AI Multi-Agent Accelerator interoperability with ServiceNow AI Agents.

name: Thoughtworks (Agent/works™)
why: Thoughtworks' Agent/works is a platform designed to address agent sprawl and governance challenges by providing a single control plane and runtime for enterprise AI agents across multi-cloud environments.
evidence: Launched Agent/works™, a platform for governing and running enterprise AI agents.
